title: Fireflies
bannerAlt: Banner image for the Fireflies Project
abstract: A vertical slice of a 3D stealth puzzle-action adventure game set within a single section of a whimsical, dark cavern environment where light is both a tool and a risk. This project was created with and for Noctua Interactive.
roleHighlights: Directed a group of four other programmers in creating a vertical slice.
Conceptualized and programmed new game mechanics from the ground up.
Collaborated across multi-disciplinary teams to meet deadlines.
Implemented UI elements and models created from other teams.

title: RTS-Roguelike
bannerAlt: Banner image for the RTS-Roguelike Project
abstract: A group project where we mixed the genres of RTS & Roguelike into a seamless innovative experience for beginner players. It's currently still under development and planning to be released in 2026!
roleHighlights: Hired and onboarded 14 people across multiple disciplines.
Coordinated group work in Basecamp for teams working in Unity, Blender, Substance Painter, and C#.
Programmed unique mechanics in Unity 6 using C#.
Conceptualized the game from the ground up.

title: Tic-Tac-Total War
cardTitle: Tic-Tac-Total War - Project Manager and Programmer
bannerAlt: Banner image for the Tic-Tac-Total War Project
abstract: This project was created as a group effort where we mixed each of our original ideas into one brand new idea! The goal of the game is to claim 3 squares in a row similar to Tic-Tac-Toe. However, the player must launch soldiers onto the board to claim said squares. They obtain soldiers through the shop with each square they claim adding to a passive income they can use to buy even more soldiers.

links.iconAlt: The logo for the Itch.io webpage.
The logo for the GitHub webpage.

roleHighlights: Managed a group of 4 other programmers working in Unity, C#, and Trello.
Organized the project within GitHub and solved any merge conflicts that arose.

title: Cowardly Blocks
bannerAlt: Banner image for the Cowardly Blocks Project
abstract: In this website I tried to create a game using RTS-like controls except in a completely different genre. The result was an interesting mix of strategy and luck in a game that gets exponentially harder.

links.iconAlt: The logo for the GitHub webpage.

roleHighlights: Programmed in HTML & Javascript using Visual Studio Code.
Designed and created from complete scratch.
Published the site onto GitHub where it is now managed.

title: The Puzzle
bannerAlt: Banner image for the The Puzzle Project
abstract: This project was created as a relatable narrative piece representing what its like deciding a major in school. The timer is there to add stress onto the user just like how it feels like there is a time limit on choosing a major.

links.iconAlt: The logo for the GitHub webpage.

roleHighlights: Programmed Javascript and HTML in Visual Studio Code
Researched on many sites such as Stack Overflow, Youtube, and Phind.
Published the site onto GitHub where it is now managed.

title: Everybody is in their own lane
cardTitle: Everybody is in their own lane - Programmer
thumbnailAlt: Everybody is in their own lane Project Thumbnail
bannerAlt: Banner image for the Everybody is in their own lane Project
abstract: In this website I created a visual digital story about the struggle of making friends. The names are randomly generated from an array and they avoid the cursor simulating a feeling of isolation for the viewer.

roleHighlights: Programmed in HTML & Javascript using Visual Studio Code.
Designed and created from complete scratch.
Published the site onto GitHub where it is now managed.

title: Payback Prank
bannerAlt: Banner image for the Tic-Tac-Total War Project
abstract: This project was created for my DTC 354 Final. I wanted to create a digital storytelling experience where the viewer would be clicking through a site similar to reddit. I made the site 'feel' functional with the ability to expand comment sections, like or dislike comments, etc.

links.iconAlt: The logo for the GitHub webpage.

roleHighlights: Programmed in Javascript and HTML on Visual Studio Code.
Project was uploaded onto GitHub and is now managed there.

title: Interactive GDD
bannerAlt: Banner image for the Interactive GDD Topic Project
abstract: This website was created to explain the current state of my game which was unnamed at the time (just called 'RTS-Roguelike'). It was first created to attract artists and 3D modelers to work on my game with me.

links.iconAlt: The logo for the GitHub webpage.

roleHighlights: Programmed in HTML & Javascript using Visual Studio Code.
Implemented code from examples online to create the background effect.
Project was uploaded onto GitHub and is now managed there.

title: 354 Hypermedia Project
bannerAlt: Banner image for the 354 Hypermedia Project
abstract: This project was created for my DTC 354 Hypermedia Project. I really wanted to simulate an environment and have the player influence said environment. It was my first stab at trying to create a morality system by demonstrating the harmful effects the player can have on their environment.
roleHighlights: Programmed in C# on Unity 6 using Visual Studio Code.
Designed and created the game from complete scratch.

title: Single Page Single Topic
thumbnailAlt: Single Page Single Topic Project Thumbnail
bannerAlt: Banner image for the Single Page Single Topic Project
abstract: This was a joke website based on a popular meme except targeted toward Minecraft modding. The goal was to program it as a single page focused on a single topic only using HTML.
